name: Laguna de la Herradura Loop
name_original: Río Jandulilla y Guadalquivir — circular Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir
description: Jog a moderate 5.8-mile (9.3 km) circular route in Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir, featuring river views and Laguna la Herradura.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Úbeda offer a blend of historical charm and natural landscapes. The region is characterized by vast olive groves, often referred to as "seas of olive trees," providing serene backdrops for running. The distant Sierra Mágina mountain range adds scenic vistas, while the Río Guadalquivir and its natural areas, such as Laguna la Herradura, offer unique environments away from the urban bustle. The historic city center, a UNESCO World Heritage site, also provides opportunities to combine physical activity with cultural exploration along ancient city walls and through Renaissance architecture. Best jogging routes around Úbeda The most popular jogging route is Úbeda City Walls Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 45 minutes to complete. This route offers historic views from the Barrio del Alcázar and along the ancient city walls. Another top favourite among local runners is Pósito Trail and Río Guadalquivir Loop, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.7 km) path. This route provides river views and a natural environment along the Guadalquivir. Local runners also love the Giribaile Reservoir Circular Route via the Ariza Bridge, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through the reservoir area, often completed in about 1 hour. Jogging around Úbeda is defined by extensive olive groves, the Río Guadalquivir, and historic city architecture. The network offers options for various ability levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Úbeda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from 4 reviews. Nearly 200 runners have used komoot to explore Úbeda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Úbeda?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Úbeda, offering a variety of experiences from urban historical paths to natural landscapes. Most of these routes are classified as moderate, with a few options for easy or more challenging runs.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on jogging trails in Úbeda?
A: Jogging in Úbeda offers a diverse range of scenery. You can run through vast 'seas of olive trees' with distant views of the Sierra Mágina, along the historic city walls showcasing Renaissance architecture, or beside natural water features like the Río Guadalquivir and reservoirs such as Embalse de Giribaile. The routes blend cultural immersion with natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Úbeda?
A: Yes, Úbeda features several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364603929/giribaile-reservoir-circular-route-via-the-ariza-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Reservoir Circular Route via the Ariza Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 6.1 miles (9.8 km) long and offers scenic water views. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367488161/laguna-de-la-herradura-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Herradura Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.8-mile (9.3 km) path through a unique natural environment.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Úbeda?
A: The running routes in Úbeda are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars. Nearly 200 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the unique blend of historical sights and tranquil natural settings, especially the olive groves and river views.

Q: Can I combine jogging with sightseeing in Úbeda?
A: Absolutely! Úbeda's status as a UNESCO World Heritage site means many running routes incorporate historical landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364645007/ubeda-city-walls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úbeda City Walls Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers historic views from the Barrio del Alcázar and along ancient city walls. You can also explore the historic center, passing through squares like Plaza Vázquez de Molina and Plaza Primero de Mayo, and admire Renaissance architecture.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for beginners in Úbeda?
A: While most routes are moderate, Úbeda does offer options suitable for beginners. The historic center provides easier paths to discover the city's charm. Look for routes with minimal elevation gain if you're just starting out, or consider shorter sections of the city-based trails.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints I can incorporate into my run?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, consider routes that pass by Úbeda's &lt;em&gt;miradores&lt;/em&gt;. The Redonda de Miradores, and the viewpoints of San Lorenzo and San Francisco, offer stunning perspectives of the city, the Sierra Mágina, and the extensive olive groves, providing motivational scenery for your run.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Úbeda?
A: The best time to enjoy running in Úbeda is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, allowing you to comfortably explore both the city's historical sites and the surrounding natural landscapes without the intense summer heat.

Q: Are there any routes that offer river views?
A: Yes, several routes provide beautiful river views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367475091/posito-trail-and-rio-guadalquivir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pósito Trail and Río Guadalquivir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.3-mile (11.7 km) path that runs along the Río Guadalquivir, offering a serene natural environment. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367488161/laguna-de-la-herradura-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Herradura Loop&lt;/a&gt; also features river views within the Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir.

Q: What attractions can I see near the running trails?
A: Many running trails in Úbeda are close to interesting attractions. Along the city routes, you can see historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6733648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de Úbeda&lt;/a&gt; and enjoy &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6263750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Alcázar District&lt;/a&gt;. If you venture towards the reservoirs, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4982802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Giribaile Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6045613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalén Reservoir and tower&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the famous olive groves?
A: Many routes around Úbeda will take you through the region's iconic 'seas of olive trees'. These vast groves provide a tranquil and picturesque backdrop for running, offering fresh air and stunning natural scenery, especially when combined with views of the distant Sierra Mágina mountain range.
tips: The point where these two well-known rivers in the area join into one and which is surrounded by agricultural fields full of olive trees.
Beautiful lagoon that is located at one end of one of the meanders of this area of the Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir, in a unique environment far from the population!

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the Río Jandulilla y Guadalquivir circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 9.3 kilometers long and typically takes around 58 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. This duration can vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Río Jandulilla y Guadalquivir circular trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate', this trail requires a good level of fitness. While it's not overly long or steep (with only about 50 meters of elevation gain), some sections may require sure-footedness. Beginners with some prior jogging experience should be able to manage it.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse, characteristic of the Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir. You can expect a mix of surfaces, potentially including unpaved dirt paths, gravel sections, and possibly some paved areas. The route winds through riparian forests, wetlands, and agricultural fields, particularly olive groves.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Río Jandulilla y Guadalquivir circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers picturesque views of the confluence of the Jandulilla River with Guadalquivir. A highlight is the tranquil La Herradura Lagoon, nestled within a river meander. You'll also pass through agricultural fields, mainly olive trees, and enjoy the diverse ecosystem of river, gallery forests, and marsh vegetation.

faqs.question: Is the Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir is a protected natural area, dogs are generally permitted on trails. However, it is crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect the local wildlife, especially birds, and to ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir?
faqs.answer: As a natural park, the Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir typically does not require an entrance fee for general access to its trails. Permits are usually not needed for activities like jogging, but it's always good practice to check local regulations for any specific activities or group visits.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to jog this trail. The weather is milder, making for more comfortable conditions. In spring, you might see more lush vegetation, while autumn offers pleasant temperatures and potentially beautiful foliage. Summers can be very hot in this region, and winters can be damp.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Río Jandulilla y Guadalquivir circular trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for this exact trailhead are not provided, but access to trails within the Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir often involves designated parking areas near visitor centers or trailheads. Look for signs indicating public parking for the natural park or specific access points to the river areas.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while jogging?
faqs.answer: The Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir is a Special Protection Area for Birds (SPA), so you have a good chance of spotting various aquatic birds, especially around the rivers and La Herradura Lagoon. The diverse ecosystem also supports other small mammals and reptiles, though they might be more elusive.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for a jog on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate jogging route, wear comfortable athletic clothing and appropriate running shoes suitable for varied terrain (some unpaved sections are likely). Bring enough water, especially during warmer months, and consider sun protection like a hat and sunscreen. A small backpack for essentials is also recommended.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Río Jandulilla y Guadalquivir — circular Paraje Natural Alto Guadalquivir' is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends at the same point without requiring you to retrace your steps.
